title: DPI Workshop DEWG
description: Second DEWG Presentation on DPI at the South Africa G20 Presidency

title: A Blueprint for Modern Digital Government
description: UK Government framework covering shared infrastructure, data, AI, and cross-government platforms for digital public services.

title: GovStack — Building Blocks for Digital Government
description: Open-source building blocks specification endorsed by ITU, GIZ, and Estonia. Modular, reusable components for digital government services.

title: Digital ID to Enhance Financial Inclusion: A Toolkit for Regulatory Authorities
description: World Bank toolkit for regulators on leveraging digital ID to deepen financial inclusion, covering risk-based KYC, tiered CDD regimes, and AML/CFT frameworks. Developed under the FIGI programme.

title: Guidelines for ID4D Diagnostics
description: Methodology for country-level diagnostics of existing and planned ID systems, covering institutions, technology, law, policy, and practice. Guided by the ten Principles on Identification for Sustainable Development.

title: MOSIP — Modular Open Source Identity Platform
description: Open-source digital ID platform deployed across 10+ countries. Modular, configurable, privacy-by-design. A leading DPG for foundational identity.

title: Data Empowerment and Protection Architecture (DEPA)
description: India's consent-based data sharing framework. Introduces Account Aggregators and a user-centric data portability model across financial, health, and other sectors.

title: Digital Health DPI Framework
description: Applies the DPI lens to national health systems — unified health IDs, health data exchanges, and facility and provider registries.

title: The Empirical Case for DPI: Evidence from LMICs
description: Synthesises evidence across 30+ countries on DPI's measurable impact on financial inclusion, service delivery, and economic growth.

title: What is Digital Public Infrastructure?
description: David Eaves and Jordan Sandman lay out a framework for understanding, designing, and deploying DPI — grounded in four principles: inclusive, foundational, interoperable, and publicly accountable.

title: How Digital Public Infrastructure Supports Empowerment, Inclusion, and Resilience
description: World Bank blog making the case for DPI as a foundation for inclusive and resilient digital economies, drawing on cross-country evidence.

title: DPI Overview — CDPI
description: Centre for Digital Public Infrastructure's concise explainer on what DPI is, how it works, and why it matters — a practical reference for practitioners.

title: The Case for Investing in Digital Public Infrastructure
description: Harvard Business Review piece arguing that DPI investment is essential for inclusive economic growth, particularly in emerging markets.

title: Explainer: What is DPI?
description: Gates Foundation accessible explainer on DPI — what it is, why governments are building it, and how it can accelerate development outcomes.

title: Government as a Platform
description: Tim O'Reilly's foundational essay arguing governments should act as platforms — providing shared infrastructure on which others build services. A precursor to modern DPI thinking.

title: G20 New Delhi Leaders' Declaration
description: The G20 New Delhi Declaration includes landmark multilateral consensus on DPI as a driver of inclusive growth — a key policy milestone for global DPI adoption.

title: G20 Digital Economy Ministers Meeting Outcome Document
description: Outcome document from the G20 Digital Economy Ministerial — covers DPI, AI governance, cybersecurity, and digital public goods as priorities for the global digital economy.

title: What is Good DPI?
description: DIAL's framework for what makes DPI good — principles and criteria for evaluating whether a DPI system is truly open, inclusive, and trustworthy.

title: The DPI Approach: A Playbook
description: UNDP and Aapti Institute playbook on implementing the DPI approach — practical guidance for governments and development practitioners on designing and deploying DPI.

title: Understanding DPI: What it Means — and Why it Matters — to Businesses and Governments in Emerging Markets
description: NextBillion piece exploring how DPI creates market opportunities and governance advantages in emerging economies, from the perspective of both public and private actors.

title: What is the DPI Approach?
description: Carnegie India piece unpacking the DPI approach — what distinguishes it from conventional digitisation and why it matters for India and other emerging economies.

title: Digital Public Goods: Enablers of Digital Sovereignty
description: OECD essay examining how digital public goods underpin digital sovereignty — covering open-source infrastructure, data governance, and the role of DPGs in national digital strategies.

title: Co-Develop: DPI for an Equitable Recovery
description: Rockefeller Foundation report making the case for DPI investment as a tool for post-pandemic equitable recovery, with evidence from multiple country deployments.

title: Promise and Pitfalls of Digital Government
description: David Eaves in The Economist on what governments get right and wrong when building digital infrastructure — and what separates successful DPI from failed digitisation.

title: Stacking up the Benefits: Lessons from India's Digital Journey
description: IMF working paper quantifying the macroeconomic and welfare benefits of India Stack — covering Aadhaar, UPI, and the broader DPI ecosystem, with lessons for other countries.

title: The Right Way to Build DPI: 5 Insights
description: World Economic Forum piece distilling five key design principles for building DPI that is truly inclusive, interoperable, and trustworthy — drawing on global case studies.

title: Best Practices for the Governance of Digital Public Goods
description: Harvard Kennedy School Ash Center report on governance models for DPGs — covering accountability, stewardship, and institutional design for open digital infrastructure.

title: Exploring Different Financing Models for DPI and Why They Matter
description: ORF research paper mapping the range of financing models for DPI — from public funding to blended finance — and their implications for sustainability and equity.

title: Why DPI Can Be a Game Changer for Children
description: WEF piece on how DPI systems — digital ID, payments, and data exchange — can unlock better outcomes for children in health, education, and social protection.

title: India Stack: Are We There Yet?
description: Dr. Pramod Varma reflects on the progress and gaps in India Stack — what has worked, what remains unfinished, and where the next frontier lies.

title: The Case for Digital Public Infrastructure
description: Ethan Zuckerman's argument for DPI as an alternative to platform capitalism — why public digital infrastructure is essential for democratic societies.

title: Government as a Platform: How Policy Makers Should Think About the Foundations of DPI
description: Harvard Kennedy School piece applying the government-as-a-platform concept to DPI — practical framing for policymakers on what to build and how to govern it.

title: DPI: The Story of India's Digital Revolution
description: Dr. RS Sharma traces India's DPI journey — from Aadhaar's early days through UPI and ONDC — and makes the case for India's model as a global template.

title: The Making of Aadhaar: World's Largest Identity Platform
description: Dr. RS Sharma's firsthand account of building Aadhaar — the world's largest biometric identity system — covering the technical, institutional, and political challenges of deploying DPI at scale.

title: Making DPI and AI Work for Welfare Services
description: Explores how combining DPI with AI can improve last-mile delivery of welfare services — with examples from India's social protection stack.

title: Addressing Climate Change with DPI
description: Co-Develop piece on how DPI systems can support climate action — from emissions monitoring and carbon markets to climate finance and disaster response.

title: An Explosion of DPI
description: Rahul Matthan's essay surveying the rapid global spread of DPI — from India Stack to Brazil's Pix — and what this proliferation means for governance and sovereignty.
